empirically_verified: Demons exist and have been documented across all six continents
Daemons exist and are distinct from lesser demons in nature, not merely scale
Demonic sites corrupt environments, people, and communities in measurable ways
Certain human beings demonstrate resistance to demonic influence that exceeds physical courage
Saint burial sites demonstrate anomalous resistance to corruption
The Pale — the First Breach — is a real geographic location of sustained demonic thinness
not_empirically_verified: Angels — no credible documented sighting in 13,003 years of recorded history
Divine intervention of any kind — no confirmed instance
Afterlife — no verified evidence for or against
The source or origin of Daemon entities
Whether Daemon nature is fixed or mutable

the_angel_absence.description: The empirical asymmetry — demons confirmed, angels absent — is the theological fault line of Arneth. Every major faith has a position on it. None can resolve it.
the_angel_absence.implications: If Hell is real and Heaven is silent, every faith must answer what that silence means. Their answers define them.
the_angel_absence.first_scholars_position: The asymmetry is data. Drawing conclusions from incomplete data is not scholarship. The investigation continues.
the_angel_absence.pale_company_position: The only verified resistance to demonic influence comes from human beings with sufficient clarity of purpose. Whether divine assistance exists is irrelevant to the work.

regional_names.name: The Grey Between
regional_names.character: Flattest, most fought-over, most politically contested no-man's-land

regional_names.name: The Thornwaste
regional_names.character: Forest that has swallowed old settlements. Trees over ruins.

regional_names.name: The Pale / The Dust Silence
regional_names.character: Vast, featureless, ruins visible from miles. The word 'Pale' here predates the Encircling Pact's use of it.

regional_names.name: The Drowning Ground
regional_names.character: Fen that has reclaimed former settlements. The ground takes things back.

regional_names.name: The Bleeding Ground
regional_names.character: Recent enough war that the name is literal. Bodies not fully recovered.

regional_names.name: The Drift
regional_names.character: Open water between island chains. Corsair Throne territory.

regional_names.name: The Long Grass
regional_names.character: Former farmland gone feral over generations. Beautiful in a way that is wrong.

regional_names.name: The Ruin Road
regional_names.character: Ancient road going nowhere, lined with collapsed structures.

regional_names.name: The Inner Dark / The Sovereign Wound
regional_names.character: The Pale itself. Most feared regional name on Arneth.

probability_weights.no_mans_lands: Highest — the absence of organized human presence allows accumulation
probability_weights.old_battlefield_sites: High — concentrated death without proper ritual resolution
probability_weights.pre_reckoning_ritual_sites: High — often unknown until manifestation begins
probability_weights.varathi_ruins: Elevated — possible connection between Varathi Collapse and demonic involvement
probability_weights.isolated_settled_territory: Low but real — historical wrong that was never addressed

warning_signs: Animals avoiding an area without clear natural reason
Crops failing in a specific geometric pattern rather than a weather or soil pattern
Ghouls appearing in a region with no recent mass death to explain them
Imp sightings — always a warning sign for something worse nearby
The specific quality the Moor Communion trains its practitioners to recognize
Saint relics behaving anomalously in proximity — warmth, resonance, light quality changes
Dreams of unusual consistency shared among people in the same area
The particular silence of animals at night in an area that should have night sounds

community_response_tiers.tier_1_local: Ghouls, imp sightings — village militia, local priest with relevant rites. Manageable with organization.
community_response_tiers.tier_2_organized: Fiend presence, active demonic site — requires the Ember Wardens, Purifiers, or Pale Company involvement. Not a local problem.
community_response_tiers.tier_3_catastrophic: Daemon activity, breach event — regional crisis. Historical record suggests these end, but the cost is significant.

demonic_taxonomy.terminology_note: Demons (lowercase) is the general category used in common speech. Daemons (capital D) is a specific and feared distinction describing ancient, named, powerful entities of a different order entirely. In rural communities near the Between, the word Daemon is spoken with a specific pause before and after it. Some communities use circumlocutions: the Named, the Old Ones, the Firsts, the Counters. A city-born character who says Daemon casually in a border village will receive a specific reaction — not anger, something older than anger.

lesser_undead.origin: Not demonic in the strict sense — these arise from concentrated death and unresolved spiritual wrong, not from a Daemon or active demonic presence. They indicate a problem. They are not the problem.

entities.name: Shades
entities.danger_level: Low in themselves — high as indicator
entities.description: Echoes of the recently dead. Not intelligent. Not purposeful. The residue of a person after sudden or unresolved death. Sailors see them in fog. The Depth faiths have specific release rites. Their presence in numbers indicates accumulated unresolved death nearby — a battlefield, a plague site, a place of mass suffering.
entities.duration: Fade within weeks to months without a sustaining source. With a demonic site nearby, they persist indefinitely.
entities.folk_belief: In the Free Fens, shades are treated with specific courtesy — addressed by their approximate age and given directions toward the ford. The Moor Communion teaches that a shade properly addressed will move on. One improperly addressed will remain.

entities.name: Ghouls
entities.description: Physical. Animated by accumulated death-energy rather than a specific demonic presence. Slow, hungry, not intelligent in any purposeful sense. The thing you find in a three-year-old battlefield that nobody properly burned or buried. Recognizable by movement pattern — they don't walk like the living walked, they move like the idea of walking without the memory of it.
entities.combat_notes: A organized village militia can handle ghouls. Alone or panicked, the danger increases significantly. Fire is effective. Dispersion of the body prevents reanimation.
entities.origin_conditions: Mass death without proper burial or burning. Plague sites. Battlefield graves that were shallow. The aftermath of famine in isolated communities.
entities.folk_belief: The Reclaimed Path teaches that proper burial rites prevent ghoul formation. This is approximately true — the rites involve deep burial, specific earth treatment, and a spoken dissolution that has genuine mild efficacy. Not supernatural. Accumulated practical knowledge encoded in religious form.

entities.name: Revenants
entities.danger_level: High — significantly more dangerous than ghouls
entities.description: A specific kind of ghoul that retains fragments of the person it was. More dangerous because it has purpose, even if the purpose is warped and incomprehensible to the living. A revenant may return to a place it knew, may recognize faces it knew, may attempt something that resembles a last action or unresolved intention. This is not comfort. A revenant that recognizes you is more dangerous than one that doesn't.
entities.origin_conditions: Unresolved death combined with intense purposeful emotion at time of death — specific grief, rage, obligation. Not all violent deaths produce revenants. The emotional quality matters as much as the circumstances.
entities.the_moor_communion_rites: The most effective documented burial practice for preventing revenancy. The Communion's rites involve specific attention to the dying person's unresolved concerns — addressing them before death, as directly as possible. The practical logic: a person who dies with nothing unresolved has less to return for.
entities.folk_belief: Rural communities throughout Arneth have specific lore about what produces revenants — dying with a spoken lie unretracted, dying with a debt uncollected, dying in a specific phase of the Arneth calendar. Most of this is inaccurate. The Moor Communion's version is the most reliable.

minor_demonic.origin: Actually demonic — something from outside the natural order is present or nearby. These arise from or near ritual sites, old catacombs, places where something was done that opened a channel.

entities.name: Imps
entities.description: Small, malicious, not particularly powerful. Dangerous in numbers or when cornered. Their primary significance is what they indicate: an active demonic site is nearby. Imps are not the source — they are the overflow. Kill all the imps in a location and the source is still there, still producing.
entities.appearance: Varied but consistent in certain qualities — small, asymmetrical, wrong in specific ways that are hard to articulate. They move like they're mocking movement. Their sounds are worse at night.
entities.behavior: Malicious without being strategic. They cause harm the way rats cause harm — not because they plan it but because their nature produces it. They will harass, bite, steal small objects, contaminate food sources, and in sufficient numbers overwhelm a person through accumulated damage.
entities.the_site_problem: A community that destroys an imp infestation without finding and sealing the source will have another infestation within weeks. The Ember Wardens' primary diagnostic skill is locating the site from imp behavior patterns.
entities.folk_belief: In the Thornwaste and other border regions, imp sightings are reported to village elders immediately. Communities with institutional memory of what imps indicate respond faster and survive better. Communities that have been peaceful too long sometimes dismiss imp sightings as small animals. This is a category error with consequences.

entities.name: Fiends
entities.danger_level: High — a regional problem requiring organized response
entities.description: A broad category covering mid-tier demonic entities. Varied in form — the taxonomy is contested because fiends do not have consistent physical presentation. What they share: intelligence sufficient for strategy, physical capability significantly beyond a human, and the specific quality of being drawn to places of prior spiritual wrong. A fiend in a ruin amplifies what is already there.
entities.intelligence: Sufficient to set traps, to understand human behavior patterns, to identify the most vulnerable member of a group and act on that identification. Not sophisticated enough for complex long-term planning. Strategic in the immediate sense.
entities.the_amplification_problem: Fiends do not simply occupy demonic sites — they worsen them. A site with a fiend present will show faster environmental corruption, more ghoul activity in the surrounding region, and increased sensitivity to demonic influence in nearby human populations. Removing the fiend without sealing the site produces a temporary improvement followed by deterioration.
entities.combat_notes: Not impossible to kill. Difficult. The Ember Wardens do it regularly. The key is not physical capability — it is not panicking when the fiend does the specific things fiends do to make people panic. Characters with high Toughness and any exposure to demonic presence perform better. Characters encountering a fiend for the first time perform poorly regardless of combat skill.
entities.folk_belief: Stories about fiends in the Between are common. Most of them are accurate in the specific detail of how to not engage — don't go into the ruin, don't follow sounds at night, don't let it get between you and the exit. The practical wisdom survives even when the theological framework doesn't.

entities.name: Incubi and Succubi
entities.danger_level: Extreme — community-level threat that is invisible until catastrophic
entities.description: Not purely physical threats. They operate through influence, corruption, and the slow erosion of judgment and will. Their presence in a community is catastrophic specifically because it is invisible. People do not know what is happening to them. By the time the pattern becomes visible — erratic behavior, judgment failures, unusual aggression or passivity, specific decisions that seem inexplicable in retrospect — multiple people have been compromised and may not be recoverable.
entities.mechanism: The influence is not magical in a simple sense. It works through the manipulation of real human needs and real human weaknesses. Fear, desire, loneliness, ambition — all real. The incubus/succubus identifies the specific vulnerability and works it over time. The corrupted person believes, entirely, that their changed behavior reflects their own choices.
entities.detection: Extremely difficult. The Pale Company's Wardens are the most reliable detectors because their specific quality of perception — seeing clearly without the distortion of personal agenda — makes the pattern visible when others cannot see it. Saint Halvard of the Deep (Tidewarden Church) is the most documented case of a human accurately identifying multiple compromised individuals. His method was attention: he paid attention to what people did rather than what they said, over time, and the pattern emerged.
the_pattern_signs: Decisions that serve no one's apparent interest, including the deciding person's
Sudden reversals of long-held positions without adequate explanation
Isolation — compromised individuals gradually withdraw from people who knew them before
A specific quality of certainty about things that don't warrant certainty
The community around a compromised individual becoming subtly worse — more fearful, more suspicious, more willing to harm
entities.recovery: Possible but not certain. Removal from the influence's source is necessary but not sufficient. The Compassionate Wound has the best documented recovery practices — their care for the dying and damaged has given them more experience with this than any other institution. The Order does not advertise this capability.
entities.the_compassionate_wound_connection: The Order's founding in the Warden States' Buffer Zone involved exactly this type of demonic threat during a period of containment weakness. Meven's documented efficacy was specifically in identifying and caring for compromised individuals. The techniques Meven taught are still practiced. That they were taught by a Daemon is the Order's central buried secret.

daemons.name: Daemons
daemons.capitalization_note: Always capitalized. The distinction from lowercase demon is not stylistic — it is categorical. A Daemon is not a large demon. It is a different order of being.
daemons.danger_level: Civilization-level threat — a confirmed active Daemon is a regional emergency

nature.description: Ancient. Named. Each one is individual in a way that lesser entities are not. They have history, agenda, and a quality of presence that everyone who has been near one and survived describes the same way: the sense that the world has become smaller and that the smaller world is paying attention to you.
nature.origin: Unknown. The First Scholars have three positions: they predate the First Reckoning, they predate the Varathi, they predate human presence on Arneth entirely. The third position is the most supported by available evidence and the most disturbing.
nature.the_naming: Every documented Daemon has a name in the historical record. Not a name given by humans — a name that appears consistently across independent accounts from different centuries and different continents. Whether Daemons name themselves or whether the name is something else entirely is not understood.
nature.fixed_nature_question: The defining theological question around Daemons is whether their nature is fixed. Standard doctrine across all faiths: yes, Daemons are what they are, evil in their essence, unchangeable. The evidence of Meven — if Meven was a Daemon — suggests otherwise. The Pale Company does not assume fixed nature. This is one of the things that makes them unusual and that makes established religious institutions deeply uncomfortable with them.

the_rural_distinction.description: In settled cities, educated people use Daemon correctly. In border villages, the Between, and rural communities near demonic sites, the word carries a different weight.
the_rural_distinction.the_pause: The word is spoken with a specific pause before and after it. Not dramatic — a small breath. The way you pause before saying the name of someone who has died.
circumlocutions: The Counters (some traditions believe Daemons count the living)
the_rural_distinction.the_city_error: A character from a city who says Daemon casually in a border village will receive a specific reaction — not anger, something more like the look people give someone who has knocked over a grave marker. The locals are not being superstitious. They are being precise about something the city person has made imprecise.

documented_daemons.count: The First Scholars' archive has six confirmed named Daemons in historical record. Three have documented last-known-active dates. Three are listed as status unknown.
documented_daemons.current_active_status: Unknown. The Pale Company believes at least one is currently active. The Archive of the Breach in Ashmark (Encircling Pact) believes two are currently active. Neither has shared this assessment with the other.
documented_daemons.the_meven_question: If Meven was a Daemon — and the evidence is strong — the documented historical record contains a Daemon who spent forty years doing genuine good. This does not change the assessment of the other five. It complicates the framework entirely.

the_first_breach.name: The Pale — also called The Sovereign Wound (Keeperhood), The First Breach (Pale Company internal), The Breathing Dark / The Old Hunger / The Watching Place / The Absence (rural communities near the Inner Zone)
the_first_breach.description: Not a demonic site in the conventional sense. Not a wound that could be healed. A thinness — the boundary between Arneth and whatever is beyond simply does not hold here the way it holds everywhere else. It predates the First Reckoning. It predates the Varathi. It may be the oldest thing on Arneth that is not Arneth.
the_first_breach.the_absence_from_maps: Does not appear on any map available outside the Encircling Pact. Regional maps show the Inner Zone as blank — no label, no description, simply absent from the cartography.
the_first_breach.current_status: The containment is degrading. This has been documented by the Archive of the Breach for 80 years. The Threshold Keepers of the Bound Marches have direct experiential knowledge of the degradation. The Pale Company's Wardens have independent assessments. None of these three have shared their findings with each other in formal terms, though there is informal overlap.

flame_traditions.position: Heaven exists but is withholding — the world must prove itself worthy through endurance and purification before divine intervention occurs.
flame_traditions.on_daemons: Instruments of divine punishment for collective impurity. The Scorched Covenant's position: the withholding is deliberate testing and demons are the test.
flame_traditions.institutional_response: The Ember Wardens — a militant wing of the Undying Ember that deals with serious manifestations. Not publicly advertised. Called in for problems that exceed local capacity.
flame_traditions.scorched_covenant_response: The Purifiers — less discriminating methods, more politically complicated. Have created community trauma that demonic influence later exploited. The Pale Company has cleaned up after Purifier actions twice.

furrow_faiths.position: The earth itself is divine. Demons are corruption of the natural order, not supernatural invasion.
furrow_faiths.on_daemons: The oldest wrong. Pre-agricultural. Pre-human. The Furrow faiths are the least theologically equipped to deal with Daemon-level events because their framework doesn't fully accommodate something that old.
furrow_faiths.institutional_response: Community-organized. The Reclaimed Path's burial rites have genuine minor efficacy against lesser undead formation. No militant wing. Relies on local organization.

depth_faiths.position: The dead go somewhere. Some come back wrong. Not all undead are demonic — some are simply lost.
depth_faiths.on_daemons: Something from the deep below the sea. Older than the water. The Tidewarden Church has specific rites for communities affected by incubus/succubus influence — developed in response to Saint Halvard of the Deep's work.
depth_faiths.institutional_response: The Tidewarden Church has the best documented recovery practices for communities affected by influence-type demonic entities. Saint Halvard's identification methods are encoded in pastoral training.

reckoning_cults.position: The First Scholars treat demonology as empirical study. The Counter-Reckoners believe the Pale is the mechanism of the coming collapse. The Varathi Return cult believes demonic involvement in the Varathi Collapse was real and that whatever the Varathi knew about managing it should be recovered.
reckoning_cults.on_daemons: The First Scholars have the most complete Daemon taxonomy in the world and the most honest assessment of what is unknown. The Varathi Return cult believes specific Daemon entities were involved in the Collapse — not as destroyers but as catalysts of something the Varathi chose to do and lost control of.
reckoning_cults.institutional_response: Scholarly documentation rather than militant response. The Archive of Keth is the best external resource for Daemon identification and historical behavior pattern analysis.

moor_communion.position: The boundary between living and dead is real and thin in certain places. What comes through from the wrong direction is not evil in a moral sense — it is wrong, the way a bone set backwards is wrong.
moor_communion.on_daemons: Something that was never on the right side of the boundary. Not a dead thing that came back wrong — something that was never alive in any Arneth sense. The Moor Communion's practitioners have the most nuanced and accurate practical knowledge of lesser demonic entities of any institutional faith. Their training in recognizing spiritual wrongness is the most reliable early warning system available.
moor_communion.institutional_response: The Saint Gorra rites — specific to the Fens, involving recognition and sealing of demonic sites. The northwest Fens contamination may be a site that Saint Gorra sealed 600 years ago that is now unsealing.

arnethi_covenant_soilborn.position: Arneth is Heaven. Demons are the assault of Hell on Heaven. Resistance is not just survival — it is devotion.
arnethi_covenant_soilborn.on_daemons: The most ancient assault. The Soilborn have no formal Daemon theology but their community response to demonic presence is the most immediate and the most practically organized of any faith, because their theology makes demonic presence a personal religious emergency rather than an institutional problem to be escalated.
arnethi_covenant_soilborn.institutional_response: Community-organized, immediate, practical. No militant wing — the whole community is the militant wing. Heavily reliant on local knowledge and speed of response. Least effective against Daemon-level events because their scale of organization doesn't match the scale of the threat.
arnethi_covenant_soilborn.relationship_with_the_pale_company: The Soilborn and the Pale Company have significant philosophical overlap — both are non-hierarchical, both trust demonstrated action over stated belief, both treat the demonic as a practical problem rather than a theological abstraction. More Pale Company Wardens came from Soilborn backgrounds than from any other faith.

keeperhood_warden_states.position: Sacred duty to contain The Pale. The Warden States are chosen for this purpose. The Pale is contained because they are holy.
keeperhood_warden_states.on_daemons: The oldest Keeperhood texts describe Daemons with empirical precision — what they are, what they do, how containment works. The current institutional Keeperhood has layered 9,000 years of theological development over those texts. Most current Keeperhood practitioners know the theology, not the original empirical practice.
keeperhood_warden_states.institutional_response: The Threshold Keepers — the genuine practitioners, concentrated in the Bound Marches. They still know what the original texts say. They are increasingly frightened by what they are seeing.
keeperhood_warden_states.the_drift_problem: The Keeperhood's current theological position — that the Pale is contained because the Warden States are holy — is functionally dangerous because it explains away signs of containment failure as tests or anomalies rather than evidence. The Threshold Keepers know this. They do not have the institutional standing to correct it.

order_of_the_compassionate_wound.position: Genuine care for the suffering, regardless of cause or faith. The theological position is almost entirely practical: suffering exists, it should be reduced, we have knowledge that helps.
order_of_the_compassionate_wound.on_daemons: The Order has the best practical knowledge of incubus/succubus influence and recovery of any institution on Arneth. This knowledge traces back to Meven. The Order's current internal division about whether to disclose Meven's nature is partly theological and partly practical — if the knowledge comes from a Daemon, does that change the knowledge?
order_of_the_compassionate_wound.the_secret: Meven was a Daemon. The Order has known this for 400 years. The evidence is suppressed but exists. The Pale Company knows. The First Scholars have fragments. The Varathi Return cult has been trying to access Meven's burial site for 60 years.
order_of_the_compassionate_wound.mevens_burial_site: The strongest anomalous saint-resistance of any documented location. Even the First Scholars acknowledge the anomaly. The Order guards it. Its location within the Warden States' territory gives the Order a specific and complicated relationship with the Encircling Pact.

institutional_militant_wings.name: The Ember Wardens
institutional_militant_wings.public_profile: Not publicly advertised. Known to exist among the educated. Called in for serious manifestations.
institutional_militant_wings.methods: Organized, experienced, effective against fiend-level threats. Work in small units. Significant institutional memory of what works.
institutional_militant_wings.relationship_with_pale_company: Cautious mutual respect. Have collaborated. Neither fully trusts the other. The Ember Wardens' institutional faith makes the Pale Company uncomfortable; the Pale Company's lack of institutional faith makes the Ember Wardens uncomfortable.

institutional_militant_wings.name: The Purifiers
institutional_militant_wings.public_profile: More visible than the Ember Wardens. Their methods are not subtle.
institutional_militant_wings.methods: Less discriminating. Have been known to harm communities they were sent to protect by treating suspected demonic influence as confirmed demonic influence before adequate investigation. The collateral damage to community cohesion creates exactly the conditions demonic influence needs to establish itself.
institutional_militant_wings.relationship_with_pale_company: Functional distrust. The Pale Company has cleaned up after Purifier actions twice. The Purifiers consider the Pale Company either a myth or a rival.
